item.title: Supporting Residents with Energy Costs and Local Services with E.ON
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
2 April 2026 Many residents across Coventry North West are continuing to feel the pressure of rising energy costs and the challenge of accessing local services. Supporting households through the cost of living crisis remains one of my top priorities. As part of this work, I recently met with E.ON to discuss how their local […]

The post Supporting Residents with Energy Costs and Local Services with E.ON first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
2 April 2026

Many residents across Coventry North West are continuing to feel the pressure of rising energy costs and the challenge of accessing local services. Supporting households through the cost of living crisis remains one of my top priorities.

As part of this work, I recently met with E.ON to discuss how their local initiatives are helping residents manage energy bills and improve long-term sustainability.

This includes:

  • Improving energy efficiency in homes, helping households reduce ongoing costs
  • Installing solar panels in local schools, including St Christopher’s, to support greener energy use
  • Working in partnership with Citizen Housing to support residents facing affordability challenges

This kind of local partnership working is essential. It not only helps bring down bills in the short term but also invests in sustainable energy solutions that will benefit our community for years to come.

There has also been some welcome short-term relief, with a 7% reduction in the energy price cap from yesterday. However, global pressures continue to affect energy markets, and prices may still fluctuate. That is why I will continue working with partners like E.ON and pressing the Government to ensure more support is available for both households and businesses.

Alongside this, improving access to local support services is just as important. I want to make sure residents know what help is available and how to access it.

If you are struggling with your energy bills or would like more information about the support available, please don’t hesitate to get in touch with my office.

The post Supporting Residents with Energy Costs and Local Services with E.ON first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: New Knife Crime Intervention Measures
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
Issue Type National Policy Issue Knifecrime Date 12 February 2026 I welcome the Government’s announcement that every child caught carrying a knife will now receive a mandatory, tailored intervention plan. We cannot arrest our way out of the knife crime crisis. If we are truly serious about saving lives, prevention, early intervention and sustained support […]

The post New Knife Crime Intervention Measures first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
Issue Type

National Policy

Issue

Knifecrime

Date

12 February 2026

I welcome the Government’s announcement that every child caught carrying a knife will now receive a mandatory, tailored intervention plan. We cannot arrest our way out of the knife crime crisis. If we are truly serious about saving lives, prevention, early intervention and sustained support for the young people who need it most must be at the heart of our response.
 
Growing up, I saw first-hand how easily vulnerable children can be groomed, exploited and drawn into gangs. I saw the fear this creates in families and the damage it does to futures. Those experiences have stayed with me and shaped my deep commitment to tackling knife crime and protecting children before they are lost to violence. This issue has always meant a great deal to me, including during my time as Chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Group on Knife Crime.
 
In Coventry, when I have spoken to local families living with the trauma of knife crime, to youth workers who go above and beyond to keep children safe, and to police officers who work tirelessly on our streets, one message comes through clearly: early and consistent support changes lives. Many young people who carry knives are frightened themselves. They are frightened of exploitation, frightened of retaliation and frightened of not belonging. If we do not confront the reasons behind that fear, we will not break the cycle.
 
Referring every young person found carrying a knife to Youth Justice Services and putting in place a targeted plan to understand and address the root causes of their behaviour is a vital step forward. It recognises that while there must be consequences, there must also be care, guidance and real opportunity.
 
The £320 million investment in Youth Justice Services is significant and welcome. Now it must translate into properly resourced local services working hand in hand with schools, families, health professionals and community organisations to intervene early and remain alongside young people for the long term.
 
Knife crime shatters families and scars communities. Behind every statistic is a child whose life could have taken a different path. I will continue to work with ministers, local partners and community leaders to ensure these measures deliver real and lasting change, because every child deserves safety, support and the chance to grow up free from fear.

Yours sincerely,

Taiwo Owatemi, 

MP for Coventry North West.

 

 

The post New Knife Crime Intervention Measures first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Visiting FANUC UK’s Exhibition in Coventry
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
29 January 2026 It was fantastic to attend the exhibition at FANUC UK alongside John Slinger and see first-hand the latest innovations in robotic precision engineering. A big thank you to Andrew for taking the time to show us around and explain the cutting-edge advances in automation, robotics and advanced manufacturing technology. From precision engineering […]

The post Visiting FANUC UK’s Exhibition in Coventry first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
29 January 2026 It was fantastic to attend the exhibition at FANUC UK alongside John Slinger and see first-hand the latest innovations in robotic precision engineering.

A big thank you to Andrew for taking the time to show us around and explain the cutting-edge advances in automation, robotics and advanced manufacturing technology. From precision engineering systems to high-performance industrial robotics, it was impressive to see how FANUC UK continues to drive innovation across the manufacturing sector.

Coventry has long been at the heart of British manufacturing, and it was great to see FANUC celebrating the city’s role as a centre of advanced manufacturing excellence. With world-class expertise, skilled engineers and forward-thinking investment, Coventry remains a leader in robotics, automation and precision engineering.

Businesses like FANUC UK are helping to strengthen the UK’s industrial future — supporting productivity, creating high-skilled jobs and ensuring British manufacturing remains globally competitive.

Thank you again for the warm welcome and the opportunity to see this innovation in action.

The post Visiting FANUC UK’s Exhibition in Coventry first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Visiting RCCG CRA Coventry in Spon End
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
SsIt was a pleasure to attend Sunday service at RCCG CRA Coventry in Spon End and experience the warmth, faith and strong community spirit that defines the congregation. RCCG CRA Coventry is an important part of the local Christian community, bringing people together for worship, fellowship and mutual support. The service was uplifting and inspiring, […]

The post Visiting RCCG CRA Coventry in Spon End first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
SsIt was a pleasure to attend Sunday service at RCCG CRA Coventry in Spon End and experience the warmth, faith and strong community spirit that defines the congregation.

RCCG CRA Coventry is an important part of the local Christian community, bringing people together for worship, fellowship and mutual support. The service was uplifting and inspiring, reflecting the church’s commitment to faith, unity and service in Coventry.

I also very much enjoyed speaking with Pastor Abiodun Olaitan at the end of the service. Faith organisations like RCCG CRA Coventry make a real difference in Spon End and across Coventry – not only as places of worship, but as hubs of community life, support and outreach.

Thank you for such a warm welcome. It was wonderful to join you and see first-hand the positive impact you continue to make.

The post Visiting RCCG CRA Coventry in Spon End first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Share Your GP Experience in Coventry North West
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
9 February 2025 Share your experience of GP services in Coventry North West Access to GP services is one of the issues raised most often by residents in Coventry North West. While there have been national efforts to increase GP appointments and reduce waiting times, it’s important to understand how these changes are being felt […]

The post Share Your GP Experience in Coventry North West first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
9 February 2025

Share your experience of GP services in Coventry North West

Access to GP services is one of the issues raised most often by residents in Coventry North West. While there have been national efforts to increase GP appointments and reduce waiting times, it’s important to understand how these changes are being felt locally and where challenges remain. That’s why I want to hear directly from residents about their experiences of GP services in Coventry North West.

Why this GP services survey matters

GP services are often the first point of contact for healthcare, and when access is difficult or care feels rushed, it can have a real impact on people’s health and wellbeing. National statistics don’t always reflect what patients experience day to day, so local feedback is essential. This survey is an opportunity for residents to share what’s working well, what isn’t, and where improvements are needed so GP services can deliver timely, high-quality care for everyone in our community.

Understanding local GP services in Coventry North West

Coventry North West has a diverse population with varying health needs. Experiences of GP services can differ widely depending on where you live, which practice you use, and your individual circumstances. By gathering feedback from across the constituency, this survey will help build a clearer picture of how GP services are really performing on the ground in Coventry North West, beyond headline figures or averages.

What the survey will ask

The survey asks about patients’ experiences of using GP services, including access to appointments, waiting times, communication with practices, and overall satisfaction with care. Both positive and negative experiences are important, as they help identify what should be protected as well as what needs to change. All responses are anonymised, and the focus is on identifying common themes rather than individual cases.

How your feedback will be used

Once the survey has closed, the anonymised results will be analysed to identify key issues and patterns. The findings will then be shared with decision-makers to ensure local experiences are taken into account. The results will be shared with the Integrated Care Board (ICB), NHS leaders, the Primary Care Minister, and the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, so that the voices of residents in Coventry North West help inform future policy and decisions about GP services.

Next steps: continuing the conversation locally

Alongside the survey, I will be holding roundtable discussions across Coventry North West in the coming months. These sessions will provide further opportunities for residents to share their experiences in more detail and discuss what improvements they would like to see. The survey is the first step in an ongoing conversation about GP services locally.

Have your say on GP services

If you live in Coventry North West and use GP services, I encourage you to take part in the survey and share your experience. Your voice matters, and your feedback can help shape improvements to GP services in our area.

Take the Survey

The post Share Your GP Experience in Coventry North West first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: National Cancer Plan
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
Issue Type National Policy Issue Healthcare Date 4 February 2026 On World Cancer Day, I joined Cancer Research UK in Parliament to mark an important moment for patients and families affected by cancer, and to welcome the government’s landmark National Cancer Plan for England. Before entering Parliament, I worked full time as an oncology pharmacist. […]

The post National Cancer Plan first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
Issue Type

National Policy

Issue

Healthcare

Date

4 February 2026

On World Cancer Day, I joined Cancer Research UK in Parliament to mark an important moment for patients and families affected by cancer, and to welcome the government’s landmark National Cancer Plan for England.

Before entering Parliament, I worked full time as an oncology pharmacist. In that role, I saw first-hand how much cancer outcomes depend on early diagnosis, timely treatment and access to the right care at the right time. I also saw how cancer never affects just one person – it affects entire families, often placing huge emotional, physical and financial strain on those supporting a loved one through treatment.

That is why this plan matters. Thanks to Labour’s record investment in our NHS, the government has set out a clear ambition to transform cancer care – with faster diagnosis, quicker treatment, and a commitment to meet all cancer waiting time standards by 2029. By 2035, three quarters of patients diagnosed will be cancer-free or living well five years on, representing the fastest improvement in cancer outcomes this century and saving hundreds of thousands of lives.

The plan sets out major reforms to how cancer is diagnosed and treated, including expanded diagnostics closer to home, wider use of genomic testing to personalise treatment, a major increase in specialist and robot-assisted surgery, and better access to clinical trials and support for people living with and beyond cancer. Every patient will receive a tailored support plan, covering not just treatment, but mental health and employment too.

I am particularly pleased to see the government’s commitment to supporting children and young people with cancer and their families. When a child is diagnosed, families often face long and frequent journeys to specialist hospitals, alongside the emotional strain of supporting a seriously ill child. More than a third of families travel over an hour to reach care, and the costs can quickly become overwhelming. The new £10 million a year fund to cover travel costs for children and young people with cancer, open to all families regardless of income, will remove one of those pressures and allow families to focus on what truly matters.

Cancer affects practically every family in every constituency. Under the Conservatives, cancer waiting time targets were missed year after year, and survival rates fell behind comparable countries. This plan represents a clear break from that failure – backed by investment, modernisation and a relentless focus on results.

The priority now is turning this ambition into real, measurable improvements for patients across the country. With faster diagnostics, cutting-edge treatment and better support for patients and families, this plan gives every person facing cancer the best possible chance of beating it – and of living well beyond it.

Yours sincerely,

Taiwo Owatemi, MP for Coventry North West.

 

 

The post National Cancer Plan first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Visiting Langar Aid in Sherbourne
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
https://videos.files.wordpress.com/1ZNyRgUR/img_3565.mov 31 January 2026 I was delighted to visit Langar Aid again in Sherbourne with my team and spend time learning more about the vital work they do to support people across our community. Rooted in the Sikh tradition of Langar – the principle of providing free food to everyone, regardless of background or circumstance […]

The post Visiting Langar Aid in Sherbourne first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
31 January 2026 I was delighted to visit Langar Aid again in Sherbourne with my team and spend time learning more about the vital work they do to support people across our community.

Rooted in the Sikh tradition of Langar – the principle of providing free food to everyone, regardless of background or circumstance — Langar Aid continues to be a lifeline for many local residents.

The Power of Langar: Free Food for All

Langar is a centuries-old Sikh practice centred on equality, dignity, and service. At Langar Aid, this tradition comes to life through the preparation and distribution of hot, nutritious meals for anyone in need.

At a time when many families are facing rising costs and financial pressures, access to reliable community food support in Sherbourne has never been more important. Langar Aid ensures that no one is turned away and that support is given with dignity and respect.

Volunteering in Action

During our visit, we were pleased to roll up our sleeves and help pack meals ready for distribution. We also had the opportunity to hand out food and speak directly with volunteers about their experiences.

These conversations were incredibly valuable. Volunteers shared insights into the growing demand for food aid locally and the challenges many individuals and families are currently facing. Their dedication and compassion were evident in every aspect of their work.

Tackling Food Insecurity in Our Community

Community organisations like Langar Aid play a crucial role in addressing food insecurity in Sherbourne and the wider area. Beyond providing meals, they offer connection, reassurance, and a sense of belonging to those who may otherwise feel isolated.

By bringing people together – whether as volunteers or recipients – Langar Aid strengthens community bonds and demonstrates the power of collective action.

A Heartfelt Thank You

Thank you to everyone at Langar Aid for such a warm welcome and for the incredible compassion and commitment you show every single day. Your work represents the very best of Sherbourne – community spirit, generosity, and the belief that everyone deserves support and respect.

The post Visiting Langar Aid in Sherbourne first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Christmas Card Competition
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
22 December 2025:  Each year, I invite pupils from schools across the constituency to take part in my Christmas Card Competition, celebrating the creativity and festive spirit of young people in our community. The competition gives children the opportunity to design a Christmas card that could be chosen as the official card sent out for […]

The post Christmas Card Competition first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
22 December 2025:  Each year, I invite pupils from schools across the constituency to take part in my Christmas Card Competition, celebrating the creativity and festive spirit of young people in our community. The competition gives children the opportunity to design a Christmas card that could be chosen as the official card sent out for the festive season.

This year, I received a fantastic range of imaginative and thoughtful entries, making it a very difficult decision. However, one design truly stood out. I was delighted to select Maksim from Radford Primary School as the overall winner for his wonderfully sweet and festive Christmas card.

Announcing the Winner at Radford Primary Academy

It was a real pleasure to visit Radford Primary Academy to announce Maksim as the winner during a special school assembly. Sharing the news with his classmates and teachers made the moment even more special.

During the assembly, I presented Maksim with his prize of three tickets to the pantomime, generously provided by The Albany Theatre. I am very grateful to The Albany Theatre for supporting local young people in our city and helping to reward Maksim’s fantastic achievement.

It was also a joy to hear the talented Nursery children sing some wonderful Christmas songs, adding to the warm and festive atmosphere. Thank you to all the pupils and staff at Radford Primary Academy for such a kind welcome.

Presenting the Winning Card at Speaker’s House

Following the school visit, I attended the Member’s Christmas Card Competition Showcase at Speaker’s House, kindly hosted by The Rt Hon Sir Lindsay Hoyle MP, Speaker of the House of Commons.

I was proud to present Maksim’s winning Christmas card design at Speaker’s House, where it was signed and prepared to be sent to him, with a copy displayed there in recognition of his achievement. It was a wonderful opportunity to celebrate his creativity and see his artwork recognised in such a special setting.

Competitions like this highlight the incredible talent, imagination and community spirit of young people across our constituency. Congratulations once again to Maksim — a very deserving winner — and thank you to every pupil who took part.

The post Christmas Card Competition first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Charity Christmas Market at Dunlop Sports & Social Club
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
14 December 2025: It was great to pop along to the charity Christmas Market at Dunlop Sports & Social Club in Coventry. The event was a fantastic opportunity for the local community to come together, enjoy the festive atmosphere, and support worthwhile causes. Events like this show the strength and generosity of our community. A […]

The post Charity Christmas Market at Dunlop Sports & Social Club first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
14 December 2025: It was great to pop along to the charity Christmas Market at Dunlop Sports & Social Club in Coventry. The event was a fantastic opportunity for the local community to come together, enjoy the festive atmosphere, and support worthwhile causes.

Events like this show the strength and generosity of our community. A big thank you to everyone who helped organise the market and to everyone who came along to make it such a special day. Your efforts are helping to raise funds for great causes while spreading holiday cheer across Coventry.

The post Charity Christmas Market at Dunlop Sports & Social Club first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.

item.title: Supporting the Street Harassment Awareness Run
item.dc:creator: taiwoo
item.description:
3 December 2025: It was wonderful to join the Street Harassment Awareness Run last month and stand alongside local runners supporting such an important initiative. Events like this play a vital role in raising awareness about street harassment and promoting safer, more inclusive spaces for everyone who enjoys running in our community. I was also […]

The post Supporting the Street Harassment Awareness Run first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
item.content:encoded:
3 December 2025: It was wonderful to join the Street Harassment Awareness Run last month and stand alongside local runners supporting such an important initiative. Events like this play a vital role in raising awareness about street harassment and promoting safer, more inclusive spaces for everyone who enjoys running in our community.

I was also pleased to help shine a wider spotlight on the event by supporting media coverage and speaking about the importance of safer streets for women and girls. I joined Vic Minett on BBC CWR, alongside the organiser Sam and fellow runners, to discuss why initiatives like this matter and how we can all play a role in tackling harassment.

Championing Safer and More Inclusive Running

A big thank you to Northbrook AC for organising the event and for their continued efforts to make running safer and more welcoming for all. Their commitment to inclusion and community safety is making a real difference.

I am also grateful to Simon Foster PCC and Coventry Police for coming along to support the cause and help raise awareness. Partnership working between community groups and local policing is key to improving safety and building confidence.

Making Our Streets Safer for Runners

Together, we are making positive strides towards safer streets for all runners. If running with others would help you feel safer, I strongly encourage you to reach out and get involved.

Northbrook AC’s buddy systems, WhatsApp groups and new group sessions are fantastic ways to stay connected, supported and safe while out on the streets. By working together as a community, we can ensure that everyone feels confident and secure when taking part in sport and exercise locally.

You can listen to me talking more about this, as well as one of the run’s organisers, here.

The segment starts at 1:13:50 and I spoke with Vic at 1:27:55

The post Supporting the Street Harassment Awareness Run first appeared on Taiwo Owatemi MP.
